 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	// MODULE: Deep-Loop Executor Config
     2	
     3	import { z } from '../../../system-spec-kit/mcp_server/node_modules/zod/index.js';
     4	
     5	// ───── TYPE DEFINITIONS ─────
     6	
     7	export const EXECUTOR_KINDS = ['native', 'cli-codex', 'cli-gemini', 'cli-claude-code', 'cli-opencode', 'cli-devin'] as const;
     8	export type ExecutorKind = typeof EXECUTOR_KINDS[number];
     9	
    10	export const REASONING_EFFORTS = ['none', 'minimal', 'low', 'medium', 'high', 'xhigh'] as const;
    11	export type ReasoningEffort = typeof REASONING_EFFORTS[number];
    12	
    13	export const SERVICE_TIERS = ['priority', 'standard', 'fast'] as const;
    14	export type ServiceTier = typeof SERVICE_TIERS[number];
    15	
    16	const SANDBOX_MODES = ['read-only', 'workspace-write', 'danger-full-access'] as const;
    17	export type SandboxMode = typeof SANDBOX_MODES[number];
    18	export type GeminiSandboxMode = 'docker' | 'none';
    19	export type ClaudePermissionMode = 'plan' | 'acceptEdits' | 'bypassPermissions';
    20	
    21	// ───── CONSTANTS ─────
    22	
    23	export const executorConfigSchema = z.object({
    24	  kind: z.enum(EXECUTOR_KINDS).default('native'),
    25	  model: z.string().min(1).nullable().default(null),
    26	  reasoningEffort: z.enum(REASONING_EFFORTS).nullable().default(null),
    27	  serviceTier: z.enum(SERVICE_TIERS).nullable().default(null),
    28	  sandboxMode: z.enum(SANDBOX_MODES).nullable().default(null),
    29	  timeoutSeconds: z.number().int().positive().default(900),
    30	});
    31	
    32	export type ExecutorConfig = z.infer<typeof executorConfigSchema>;
    33	
    34	export const EXECUTOR_KIND_FLAG_SUPPORT: Record<ExecutorKind, readonly (keyof ExecutorConfig)[]> = {
    35	  native: [],
    36	  'cli-codex': ['model', 'reasoningEffort', 'serviceTier', 'sandboxMode', 'timeoutSeconds'],
    37	  'cli-gemini': ['model', 'sandboxMode', 'timeoutSeconds'],
    38	  'cli-claude-code': ['model', 'reasoningEffort', 'sandboxMode', 'timeoutSeconds'],
    39	  'cli-opencode': ['model', 'reasoningEffort', 'timeoutSeconds'],
    40	  'cli-devin': ['model', 'sandboxMode', 'timeoutSeconds'],
    41	};
    42	
    43	export const GEMINI_SUPPORTED_MODELS = ['gemini-3.1-pro-preview'] as const;
    44	export type GeminiSupportedModel = typeof GEMINI_SUPPORTED_MODELS[number];
    45	
    46	export const DEVIN_SUPPORTED_MODELS = ['swe-1.6', 'deepseek-v4', 'glm-5.1', 'kimi-k2.6'] as const;
    47	export type DevinSupportedModel = typeof DEVIN_SUPPORTED_MODELS[number];
    48	export type DevinPermissionMode = 'auto' | 'dangerous';
    49	
    50	// ───── DOMAIN ERRORS ─────
    51	
    52	type ExecutorConfigIssue = {
    53	  path: PropertyKey[];
    54	  message: string;
    55	};
    56	
    57	export class ExecutorNotWiredError extends Error {
    58	  kind: Extract<ExecutorKind, 'cli-gemini' | 'cli-claude-code'>;
    59	
    60	  constructor(kind: Extract<ExecutorKind, 'cli-gemini' | 'cli-claude-code'>) {
    61	    super(`Executor kind '${kind}' is reserved in the schema but not yet wired. Awaiting future spec for ${kind} integration.`);
    62	    this.name = 'ExecutorNotWiredError';
    63	    this.kind = kind;
    64	  }
    65	}
    66	
    67	export class ExecutorConfigError extends Error {
    68	  issues: ExecutorConfigIssue[];
    69	
    70	  constructor(input: { issues: ExecutorConfigIssue[] }) {
    71	    super(input.issues.map((issue) => `${issue.path.map(String).join('.') || '<root>'}: ${issue.message}`).join('; '));
    72	    this.name = 'ExecutorConfigError';
    73	    this.issues = input.issues;
    74	  }
    75	}
    76	
    77	// ───── HELPERS ─────
    78	
    79	function isRecord(value: unknown): value is Record<string, unknown> {
    80	  return value !== null && !Array.isArray(value) && typeof value === 'object';
    81	}
    82	
    83	function normalizeSandboxMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): SandboxMode {
    84	  return mode ?? 'workspace-write';
    85	}
    86	
    87	function normalizeExecutorConfigInput(raw: unknown): unknown {
    88	  if (!isRecord(raw) || !Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(raw, 'type')) {
    89	    return raw;
    90	  }
    91	
    92	  const legacyKind = raw.type;
    93	  if (typeof legacyKind !== 'string') {
    94	    return raw;
    95	  }
    96	
    97	  if (typeof raw.kind === 'string' && raw.kind !== legacyKind) {
    98	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({
    99	      issues: [{
   100	        path: ['type'],
   101	        message: `deprecated executor field 'type' conflicts with canonical kind '${raw.kind}'`,
   102	      }],
   103	    });
   104	  }
   105	
   106	  console.warn("[executor-config] Deprecated executor field 'type' was provided; use 'kind' instead.");
   107	  const { type: _legacyType, ...rest } = raw;
   108	  return {
   109	    ...rest,
   110	    kind: typeof raw.kind === 'string' ? raw.kind : legacyKind,
   111	  };
   112	}
   113	
   114	function normalizeIssues(error: z.ZodError<ExecutorConfig>): ExecutorConfigIssue[] {
   115	  return error.issues.map((issue) => ({
   116	    path: issue.path,
   117	    message: issue.message,
   118	  }));
   119	}
   120	
   121	// ───── EXPORTS ─────
   122	
   123	/**
   124	 * Map a generic sandbox mode to the Codex CLI sandbox mode.
   125	 *
   126	 * @param mode - Generic sandbox mode or undefined.
   127	 * @returns Codex-compatible sandbox mode.
   128	 */
   129	export function resolveCodexSandboxMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): SandboxMode {
   130	  return normalizeSandboxMode(mode);
   131	}
   132	
   133	/**
   134	 * Map a generic sandbox mode to the Gemini CLI sandbox mode.
   135	 *
   136	 * @param mode - Generic sandbox mode or undefined.
   137	 * @returns 'docker' for workspace-write/read-only, 'none' for danger-full-access.
   138	 */
   139	export function resolveGeminiSandboxMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): GeminiSandboxMode {
   140	  return normalizeSandboxMode(mode) === 'danger-full-access' ? 'none' : 'docker';
   141	}
   142	
   143	/**
   144	 * Map a generic sandbox mode to the Claude Code permission mode.
   145	 *
   146	 * @param mode - Generic sandbox mode or undefined.
   147	 * @returns Claude-compatible permission mode.
   148	 */
   149	export function resolveClaudePermissionMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): ClaudePermissionMode {
   150	  switch (normalizeSandboxMode(mode)) {
   151	    case 'read-only':
   152	      return 'plan';
   153	    case 'danger-full-access':
   154	      return 'bypassPermissions';
   155	    default:
   156	      return 'acceptEdits';
   157	  }
   158	}
   159	
   160	/**
   161	 * Map a generic sandbox mode to the Devin CLI permission mode.
   162	 *
   163	 * @param mode - Generic sandbox mode or undefined.
   164	 * @returns 'auto' for workspace-write/read-only, 'dangerous' for danger-full-access.
   165	 */
   166	export function resolveDevinPermissionMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): DevinPermissionMode {
   167	  return normalizeSandboxMode(mode) === 'danger-full-access' ? 'dangerous' : 'auto';
   168	}
   169	
   170	/**
   171	 * Parse and validate a raw executor configuration.
   172	 *
   173	 * Normalizes legacy `type` fields, validates against the Zod schema,
   174	 * and enforces executor-kind-specific field support.
   175	 *
   176	 * @param raw - Raw input to parse (JSON-parsed object).
   177	 * @returns Validated ExecutorConfig.
   178	 * @throws {@link ExecutorConfigError} If validation fails.
   179	 */
   180	export function parseExecutorConfig(raw: unknown): ExecutorConfig {
   181	  const parsed = executorConfigSchema.safeParse(normalizeExecutorConfigInput(raw));
   182	  if (!parsed.success) {
   183	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   184	      issues: normalizeIssues(parsed.error),
   185	    });
   186	  }
   187	
   188	  const config = parsed.data;
   189	  if (config.kind === 'cli-codex' && config.model === null) {
   190	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   191	      issues: [{ path: ['model'], message: 'model is required when kind is cli-codex' }],
   192	    });
   193	  }
   194	  if (config.kind === 'cli-devin' && config.model === null) {
   195	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   196	      issues: [{ path: ['model'], message: 'model is required when kind is cli-devin' }],
   197	    });
   198	  }
   199	
   200	  const supportedFlags = EXECUTOR_KIND_FLAG_SUPPORT[config.kind];
   201	  const unsupportedFields: string[] = [];
   202	  const allOptionalFields: (keyof ExecutorConfig)[] = [
   203	    'model',
   204	    'reasoningEffort',
   205	    'serviceTier',
   206	    'sandboxMode',
   207	    'timeoutSeconds',
   208	  ];
   209	
   210	  for (const field of allOptionalFields) {
   211	    if (!supportedFlags.includes(field) && config[field] !== null && !(field === 'timeoutSeconds' && config[field] === 900)) {
   212	      unsupportedFields.push(field);
   213	    }
   214	  }
   215	
   216	  if (unsupportedFields.length > 0) {
   217	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   218	      issues: unsupportedFields.map((field) => ({
   219	        path: [field],
   220	        message: `field '${field}' is not supported by executor kind '${config.kind}'. Supported fields for ${config.kind}: ${
   221	          supportedFlags.length ? supportedFlags.join(', ') : 'none'
   222	        }.`,
   223	      })),
   224	    });
   225	  }
   226	
   227	  if (
   228	    config.kind === 'cli-gemini' &&
   229	    config.model !== null &&
   230	    !GEMINI_SUPPORTED_MODELS.some((model) => model === config.model)
   231	  ) {
   232	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   233	      issues: [
   234	        {
   235	          path: ['model'],
   236	          message: `model '${config.model}' is not a supported cli-gemini model. Supported: ${GEMINI_SUPPORTED_MODELS.join(', ')}.`,
   237	        },
   238	      ],
   239	    });
   240	  }
   241	
   242	  if (
   243	    config.kind === 'cli-devin' &&
   244	    config.model !== null &&
   245	    !DEVIN_SUPPORTED_MODELS.some((model) => model === config.model)
   246	  ) {
   247	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   248	      issues: [
   249	        {
   250	          path: ['model'],
   251	          message: `model '${config.model}' is not a supported cli-devin model. Supported: ${DEVIN_SUPPORTED_MODELS.join(', ')}.`,
   252	        },
   253	      ],
   254	    });
   255	  }
   256	
   257	  return config;
   258	}
   259	
   260	/**
   261	 * Resolve an executor configuration by merging CLI and file sources.
   262	 *
   263	 * @param sources - CLI and file partial configs (CLI takes precedence).
   264	 * @returns Validated ExecutorConfig.
   265	 */
   266	export function resolveExecutorConfig(sources: {
   267	  cli?: Partial<ExecutorConfig>;
   268	  file?: Partial<ExecutorConfig>;
   269	}): ExecutorConfig {
   270	  const merged = {
   271	    ...(sources.file ?? {}),
   272	    ...(sources.cli ?? {}),
   273	  };
   274	  return parseExecutorConfig(merged);
   275	}
   276	
   277	// ───── FAN-OUT (MULTI-EXECUTOR) CONFIG ─────
   278	//
   279	// Opt-in layer ABOVE the single-executor path. When a fan-out config is present,
   280	// the loop runs N executor "lineages" concurrently (capped), each running the
   281	// existing sequential loop in its own isolated sub-packet. The single-executor
   282	// path (executorConfigSchema / parseExecutorConfig) is untouched and remains the
   283	// default; callers use EITHER a single `executor` OR a `fanout` block, never both.
   284	
   285	const LINEAGE_LABEL_PATTERN = /^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$/;
   286	
   287	/**
   288	 * One fan-out lineage: a single executor config plus fan-out metadata.
   289	 *
   290	 * - `label`: directory-safe id for the lineage's isolated sub-packet.
   291	 * - `count`: number of replicas of THIS config to run (e.g. 5x the same model).
   292	 * - `iterations`: per-lineage max-iterations override; null = packet default.
   293	 */
   294	export const lineageExecutorSchema = executorConfigSchema.extend({
   295	  label: z.string().min(1).regex(LINEAGE_LABEL_PATTERN, {
   296	    message: "label must match /^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$/ (lowercase, digits, hyphens; dir-safe)",
   297	  }),
   298	  count: z.number().int().positive().default(1),
   299	  iterations: z.number().int().positive().nullable().default(null),
   300	});
   301	
   302	export type LineageExecutor = z.infer<typeof lineageExecutorSchema>;
   303	
   304	export const fanoutConfigSchema = z.object({
   305	  executors: z.array(lineageExecutorSchema).min(1),
   306	  concurrency: z.number().int().positive().default(2),
   307	});
   308	
   309	export type FanoutConfig = z.infer<typeof fanoutConfigSchema>;
   310	
   311	/**
   312	 * Parse and validate a raw fan-out configuration.
   313	 *
   314	 * Each entry's executor subset is routed through {@link parseExecutorConfig} so
   315	 * ALL existing kind/model/flag rules (cli-codex needs model, gemini/devin model
   316	 * whitelists, per-kind flag support) apply per lineage with zero duplication.
   317	 * Labels must be unique and base-label (count>1) expansion must not collide.
   318	 *
   319	 * @param raw - Raw input to parse (JSON-parsed object).
   320	 * @returns Validated FanoutConfig.
   321	 * @throws {@link ExecutorConfigError} If validation fails.
   322	 */
   323	export function parseFanoutConfig(raw: unknown): FanoutConfig {
   324	  const parsed = fanoutConfigSchema.safeParse(raw);
   325	  if (!parsed.success) {
   326	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({ issues: parsed.error.issues.map((issue) => ({ path: issue.path, message: issue.message })) });
   327	  }
   328	
   329	  const config = parsed.data;
   330	
   331	  // Reuse the canonical single-executor validator per entry (kind/model/flags).
   332	  config.executors.forEach((entry, index) => {
   333	    const { label: _label, count: _count, iterations: _iterations, ...executorSubset } = entry;
   334	    try {
   335	      parseExecutorConfig(executorSubset);
   336	    } catch (err) {
   337	      if (err instanceof ExecutorConfigError) {
   338	        throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   339	          issues: err.issues.map((issue) => ({ path: ['executors', index, ...issue.path], message: issue.message })),
   340	        });
   341	      }
   342	      throw err;
   343	    }
   344	  });
   345	
   346	  // Labels must be unique among the declared lineages.
   347	  const seenLabels = new Set<string>();
   348	  config.executors.forEach((entry, index) => {
   349	    if (seenLabels.has(entry.label)) {
   350	      throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   351	        issues: [{ path: ['executors', index, 'label'], message: `duplicate lineage label '${entry.label}'` }],
   352	      });
   353	    }
   354	    seenLabels.add(entry.label);
   355	  });
   356	
   357	  // Expanded labels (count>1 → label-1, label-2, …) must not collide either.
   358	  const expandedLabels = new Set<string>();
   359	  for (const lineage of expandLineages(config)) {
   360	    if (expandedLabels.has(lineage.label)) {
   361	      throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   362	        issues: [{ path: ['executors'], message: `expanded lineage label '${lineage.label}' collides; rename base labels` }],
   363	      });
   364	    }
   365	    expandedLabels.add(lineage.label);
   366	  }
   367	
   368	  return config;
   369	}
   370	
   371	/**
   372	 * Expand a fan-out config into concrete per-replica lineages.
   373	 *
   374	 * A `count` of 1 keeps the base label; `count > 1` yields `${label}-1` …
   375	 * `${label}-N`. Each expanded lineage carries `count: 1` so downstream
   376	 * consumers see one runnable unit per element.
   377	 *
   378	 * @param config - Validated FanoutConfig.
   379	 * @returns Flat list of single-replica lineages (one runnable unit each).
   380	 */
   381	export function expandLineages(config: FanoutConfig): LineageExecutor[] {
   382	  const lineages: LineageExecutor[] = [];
   383	  for (const entry of config.executors) {
   384	    if (entry.count === 1) {
   385	      lineages.push({ ...entry, count: 1 });
   386	      continue;
   387	    }
   388	    for (let replica = 1; replica <= entry.count; replica += 1) {
   389	      lineages.push({ ...entry, label: `${entry.label}-${replica}`, count: 1 });
   390	    }
   391	  }
   392	  return lineages;
   393	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	// MODULE: Deep-Loop Executor Audit
     2	
     3	import { spawn, spawnSync } from 'node:child_process';
     4	import { existsSync, readFileSync, readdirSync, statSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs';
     5	import { basename, join } from 'node:path';
     6	
     7	import type { ExecutorConfig, ExecutorKind } from './executor-config.js';
     8	import { appendJsonlRecord as appendJsonlRecordSafe, repairJsonlTail } from './jsonl-repair.js';
     9	
    10	// ───── TYPE DEFINITIONS ─────
    11	
    12	export const CLI_DISPATCH_STACK_ENV = 'SPECKIT_CLI_DISPATCH_STACK' as const;
    13	
    14	export type DispatchFailureReason =
    15	  | 'timeout'
    16	  | 'crash'
    17	  | 'missing_output'
    18	  | 'invalid_output'
    19	  | 'other'
    20	  | RecursionGuardFailureReason;
    21	
    22	export type RecursionGuardLayer = 'stack' | 'ancestry' | 'env' | 'lockfile';
    23	export type RecursionGuardFailureReason =
    24	  | 'recursion-guard-stack'
    25	  | 'recursion-guard-ancestry'
    26	  | 'recursion-guard-env'
    27	  | 'recursion-guard-lockfile';
    28	
    29	export type ExecutorDispatchAllowedResult =
    30	  | { allowed: true }
    31	  | {
    32	      allowed: false;
    33	      reason: RecursionGuardFailureReason;
    34	      layer: RecursionGuardLayer;
    35	      detail: string;
    36	    };
    37	
    38	// ───── CONSTANTS ─────
    39	
    40	type ExecutorDispatchGuardContext = {
    41	  env?: Record<string, string | undefined>;
    42	  ancestryCmdlines?: string[];
    43	  statePaths?: string[];
    44	};
    45	
    46	const EXECUTOR_BINARY_BY_KIND: Partial<Record<ExecutorKind, string>> = {
    47	  'cli-codex': 'codex',
    48	  'cli-gemini': 'gemini',
    49	  'cli-claude-code': 'claude',
    50	  'cli-opencode': 'opencode',
    51	  'cli-devin': 'devin',
    52	};
    53	
    54	const EXECUTOR_SESSION_ENV_BY_KIND: Partial<Record<ExecutorKind, string>> = {
    55	  'cli-codex': 'CODEX_SESSION_ID',
    56	  'cli-gemini': 'GEMINI_SESSION_ID',
    57	  'cli-claude-code': 'CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID',
    58	  'cli-opencode': 'OPENCODE_SESSION_ID',
    59	  'cli-devin': 'DEVIN_SESSION_ID',
    60	};
    61	
    62	const EXECUTOR_STATE_ENV_BY_KIND: Partial<Record<ExecutorKind, string[]>> = {
    63	  'cli-codex': ['SPECKIT_CODEX_STATE_DIR', 'CODEX_HOME'],
    64	  'cli-gemini': ['SPECKIT_GEMINI_STATE_DIR', 'GEMINI_HOME'],
    65	  'cli-claude-code': ['SPECKIT_CLAUDE_CODE_STATE_DIR', 'CLAUDE_CODE_HOME', 'CLAUDE_HOME'],
    66	  'cli-opencode': ['SPECKIT_OPENCODE_STATE_DIR', 'OPENCODE_HOME'],
    67	  'cli-devin': ['SPECKIT_DEVIN_STATE_DIR', 'DEVIN_HOME'],
    68	};
    69	
    70	const EXECUTOR_DEFAULT_HOME_DIR_BY_KIND: Partial<Record<ExecutorKind, string>> = {
    71	  'cli-codex': '.codex',
    72	  'cli-gemini': '.gemini',
    73	  'cli-claude-code': '.claude',
    74	  'cli-opencode': '.opencode',
    75	  'cli-devin': '.devin',
    76	};
    77	
    78	const EXECUTOR_COMMON_ENV_ALLOWLIST = new Set([
    79	  'PATH',
    80	  'HOME',
    81	  'LANG',
    82	  'TMPDIR',
    83	  'NODE_PATH',
    84	  'TERM',
    85	]);
    86	
    87	const EXECUTOR_ENV_PREFIXES_BY_KIND: Partial<Record<ExecutorKind, string[]>> = {
    88	  'cli-codex': ['CODEX_', 'OPENAI_', 'AZURE_OPENAI_'],
    89	  'cli-gemini': ['GEMINI_', 'GOOGLE_'],
    90	  'cli-claude-code': ['CLAUDE_', 'CLAUDE_CODE_', 'ANTHROPIC_'],
    91	  'cli-opencode': ['OPENCODE_'],
    92	  'cli-devin': ['DEVIN_'],
    93	};
    94	
    95	type RunAuditedExecutorCommandInput = {
    96	  command: string;
    97	  args: string[];
    98	  cwd: string;
    99	  timeoutSeconds: number;
   100	  stateLogPath: string;
   101	  executor: ExecutorConfig;
   102	  iteration: number;
   103	  input?: string;
   104	  guardContext?: ExecutorDispatchGuardContext;
   105	  timeoutGraceMs?: number;
   106	  lineageId?: string;
   107	};
   108	
   109	// ───── HELPERS ─────
   110	
   111	function getExecutorKind(config: ExecutorConfig): ExecutorKind {
   112	  return config.kind ?? (config as ExecutorConfig & { type?: ExecutorKind }).type ?? 'native';
   113	}
   114	
   115	function isAllowedExecutorEnvKey(key: string, kind: ExecutorKind): boolean {
   116	  if (EXECUTOR_COMMON_ENV_ALLOWLIST.has(key) || key.startsWith('LC_')) {
   117	    return true;
   118	  }
   119	  return (EXECUTOR_ENV_PREFIXES_BY_KIND[kind] ?? []).some((prefix) => key.startsWith(prefix));
   120	}
   121	
   122	function recursionReasonForLayer(layer: RecursionGuardLayer): RecursionGuardFailureReason {
   123	  return `recursion-guard-${layer}` as RecursionGuardFailureReason;
   124	}
   125	
   126	function splitDispatchStack(stack: string | undefined): string[] {
   127	  return (stack ?? '')
   128	    .split(':')
   129	    .map((entry) => entry.trim())
   130	    .filter((entry) => entry.length > 0);
   131	}
   132	
   133	function commandLineContainsBinary(commandLine: string, binary: string): boolean {
   134	  return commandLine
   135	    .split(/\0|\s+/)
   136	    .map((token) => token.trim().replace(/^['"]|['"]$/g, ''))
   137	    .filter((token) => token.length > 0)
   138	    .some((token) => basename(token) === binary);
   139	}
   140	
   141	function readLinuxAncestorCmdlines(startPid: number = process.ppid): string[] {
   142	  const cmdlines: string[] = [];
   143	  let currentPid = startPid;
   144	  const seen = new Set<number>();
   145	
   146	  while (currentPid > 1 && !seen.has(currentPid)) {
   147	    seen.add(currentPid);
   148	    const procDir = join('/proc', String(currentPid));
   149	    const cmdlinePath = join(procDir, 'cmdline');
   150	    const statPath = join(procDir, 'stat');
   151	
   152	    if (!existsSync(cmdlinePath) || !existsSync(statPath)) {
   153	      break;
   154	    }
   155	
   156	    try {
   157	      const cmdline = readFileSync(cmdlinePath, 'utf8').replace(/\0/g, ' ').trim();
   158	      if (cmdline.length > 0) {
   159	        cmdlines.push(cmdline);
   160	      }
   161	
   162	      const stat = readFileSync(statPath, 'utf8');
   163	      const closeParenIndex = stat.lastIndexOf(')');
   164	      const afterCommand = closeParenIndex === -1 ? '' : stat.slice(closeParenIndex + 2).trim();
   165	      const parentPid = Number(afterCommand.split(/\s+/)[1]);
   166	      if (!Number.isInteger(parentPid) || parentPid <= 0) {
   167	        break;
   168	      }
   169	      currentPid = parentPid;
   170	    } catch {
   171	      break;
   172	    }
   173	  }
   174	
   175	  return cmdlines;
   176	}
   177	
   178	function getDefaultStatePaths(kind: ExecutorKind, env: Record<string, string | undefined>): string[] {
   179	  const paths: string[] = [];
   180	  for (const envName of EXECUTOR_STATE_ENV_BY_KIND[kind] ?? []) {
   181	    const value = env[envName];
   182	    if (typeof value === 'string' && value.trim() !== '') {
   183	      paths.push(value);
   184	    }
   185	  }
   186	
   187	  const home = env.HOME;
   188	  const homeDir = EXECUTOR_DEFAULT_HOME_DIR_BY_KIND[kind];
   189	  if (typeof home === 'string' && homeDir) {
   190	    paths.push(join(home, homeDir));
   191	  }
   192	
   193	  return [...new Set(paths)];
   194	}
   195	
   196	function candidateLockfileNames(kind: ExecutorKind): string[] {
   197	  const binary = EXECUTOR_BINARY_BY_KIND[kind] ?? kind;
   198	  return [
   199	    `${kind}.lock`,
   200	    `${binary}.lock`,
   201	    `speckit-${kind}.lock`,
   202	    `speckit-cli-dispatch-${kind}.lock`,
   203	  ];
   204	}
   205	
   206	function pathIsReadableDirectory(path: string): boolean {
   207	  try {
   208	    return statSync(path).isDirectory();
   209	  } catch {
   210	    return false;
   211	  }
   212	}
   213	
   214	function findLastNonEmptyLineIndex(lines: string[]): number {
   215	  for (let index = lines.length - 1; index >= 0; index -= 1) {
   216	    if (lines[index]?.trim() !== '') {
   217	      return index;
   218	    }
   219	  }
   220	
   221	  return -1;
   222	}
   223	
   224	function isObjectRecord(value: unknown): value is Record<string, unknown> {
   225	  return value !== null && !Array.isArray(value) && typeof value === 'object';
   226	}
   227	
   228	function readJsonlFile(stateLogPath: string): {
   229	  content: string;
   230	  hasTrailingNewline: boolean;
   231	  lines: string[];
   232	} {
   233	  const content = readFileSync(stateLogPath, 'utf8');
   234	  return {
   235	    content,
   236	    hasTrailingNewline: content.endsWith('\n'),
   237	    lines: content.split(/\r?\n/),
   238	  };
   239	}
   240	
   241	function readJsonlFileAfterTailRepair(stateLogPath: string): ReturnType<typeof readJsonlFile> {
   242	  repairJsonlTail(stateLogPath);
   243	  return readJsonlFile(stateLogPath);
   244	}
   245	
   246	function rewriteJsonlFile(stateLogPath: string, lines: string[], hasTrailingNewline: boolean): void {
   247	  const rewritten = lines.join('\n');
   248	  writeFileSync(stateLogPath, hasTrailingNewline ? `${rewritten}\n` : rewritten, 'utf8');
   249	}
   250	
   251	function parseJsonlObjectLine(line: string): Record<string, unknown> | null {
   252	  try {
   253	    const parsedRecord = JSON.parse(line);
   254	    return isObjectRecord(parsedRecord) ? parsedRecord : null;
   255	  } catch {
   256	    return null;
   257	  }
   258	}
   259	
   260	function findLatestIterationRecordIndex(lines: string[], iteration: number): number {
   261	  for (let index = lines.length - 1; index >= 0; index -= 1) {
   262	    const line = lines[index]?.trim();
   263	    if (!line) {
   264	      continue;
   265	    }
   266	
   267	    const parsedRecord = parseJsonlObjectLine(line);
   268	    if (
   269	      parsedRecord &&
   270	      parsedRecord.iteration === iteration &&
   271	      (parsedRecord.type === 'iteration' || parsedRecord.type === 'iteration_start')
   272	    ) {
   273	      return index;
   274	    }
   275	  }
   276	
   277	  return -1;
   278	}
   279	
   280	function findLatestIterationEvent(lines: string[], iteration: number): Record<string, unknown> | null {
   281	  for (let index = lines.length - 1; index >= 0; index -= 1) {
   282	    const line = lines[index]?.trim();
   283	    if (!line) {
   284	      continue;
   285	    }
   286	
   287	    const parsedRecord = parseJsonlObjectLine(line);
   288	    if (
   289	      parsedRecord &&
   290	      parsedRecord.iteration === iteration &&
   291	      parsedRecord.type === 'event'
   292	    ) {
   293	      return parsedRecord;
   294	    }
   295	  }
   296	
   297	  return null;
   298	}
   299	
   300	function killProcessGroup(pid: number | undefined, signal: NodeJS.Signals): void {
   301	  if (typeof pid !== 'number') {
   302	    return;
   303	  }
   304	
   305	  try {
   306	    process.kill(process.platform === 'win32' ? pid : -pid, signal);
   307	  } catch {
   308	  }
   309	}
   310	
   311	// ───── EXPORTS ─────
   312	
   313	/**
   314	 * Detect whether the same executor kind already appears in the dispatch stack.
   315	 *
   316	 * @param stack - The SPECKIT_CLI_DISPATCH_STACK env value.
   317	 * @param kind - The executor kind to check.
   318	 * @returns True if the kind is already present in the stack.
   319	 */
   320	export function detectSameKindFromStack(stack: string | undefined, kind: ExecutorKind): boolean {
   321	  if (kind === 'native') {
   322	    return false;
   323	  }
   324	
   325	  return splitDispatchStack(stack).includes(kind);
   326	}
   327	
   328	/**
   329	 * Detect whether an executor binary appears in the process ancestry.
   330	 *
   331	 * Reads Linux /proc ancestor cmdlines to find the executor binary.
   332	 *
   333	 * @param kind - The executor kind to check.
   334	 * @param ancestryCmdlines - Pre-computed ancestor command lines (optional).
   335	 * @returns True if the executor binary is found in the ancestry.
   336	 */
   337	export function detectFromAncestry(kind: ExecutorKind, ancestryCmdlines: string[] = readLinuxAncestorCmdlines()): boolean {
   338	  const binary = EXECUTOR_BINARY_BY_KIND[kind];
   339	  if (!binary) {
   340	    return false;
   341	  }
   342	
   343	  return ancestryCmdlines.some((commandLine) => commandLineContainsBinary(commandLine, binary));
   344	}
   345	
   346	/**
   347	 * Detect whether a runtime session environment variable is set for an executor.
   348	 *
   349	 * @param kind - The executor kind to check.
   350	 * @param env - Environment variable map (defaults to process.env).
   351	 * @returns True if the session environment variable is set.
   352	 */
   353	export function detectFromRuntimeEnv(
   354	  kind: ExecutorKind,
   355	  env: Record<string, string | undefined> = process.env,
   356	): boolean {
   357	  const sessionEnvName = EXECUTOR_SESSION_ENV_BY_KIND[kind];
   358	  if (!sessionEnvName) {
   359	    return false;
   360	  }
   361	
   362	  return typeof env[sessionEnvName] === 'string' && env[sessionEnvName]?.trim() !== '';
   363	}
   364	
   365	/**
   366	 * Detect whether a dispatch lockfile exists in executor state directories.
   367	 *
   368	 * @param kind - The executor kind to check.
   369	 * @param statePaths - State directory paths to scan (defaults to auto-detected).
   370	 * @returns True if a lockfile is found.
   371	 */
   372	export function detectFromLockfile(kind: ExecutorKind, statePaths: string[] = getDefaultStatePaths(kind, process.env)): boolean {
   373	  if (kind === 'native') {
   374	    return false;
   375	  }
   376	
   377	  const names = candidateLockfileNames(kind);
   378	  for (const statePath of statePaths) {
   379	    const directories = [statePath, join(statePath, 'locks')];
   380	    for (const directory of directories) {
   381	      if (!pathIsReadableDirectory(directory)) {
   382	        continue;
   383	      }
   384	      const entries = new Set(readdirSync(directory));
   385	      if (names.some((name) => entries.has(name))) {
   386	        return true;
   387	      }
   388	    }
   389	  }
   390	
   391	  return false;
   392	}
   393	
   394	/**
   395	 * Validate that an executor dispatch is allowed.
   396	 *
   397	 * Checks stack, ancestry, runtime env, and lockfile layers for recursion
   398	 * guard violations per the deep-loop dispatch contract.
   399	 *
   400	 * @param config - Executor configuration.
   401	 * @param context - Optional environment, ancestry, and state path context.
   402	 * @returns Dispatch result indicating whether dispatch is allowed.
   403	 */
   404	export function validateExecutorDispatchAllowed(
   405	  config: ExecutorConfig,
   406	  context: ExecutorDispatchGuardContext = {},
   407	): ExecutorDispatchAllowedResult {
   408	  const kind = getExecutorKind(config);
   409	  if (kind === 'native') {
   410	    return { allowed: true };
   411	  }
   412	
   413	  const env = context.env ?? process.env;
   414	  const stack = env[CLI_DISPATCH_STACK_ENV];
   415	  if (detectSameKindFromStack(stack, kind)) {
   416	    return {
   417	      allowed: false,
   418	      layer: 'stack',
   419	      reason: recursionReasonForLayer('stack'),
   420	      detail: `${kind} already appears in ${CLI_DISPATCH_STACK_ENV}`,
   421	    };
   422	  }
   423	
   424	  if (detectFromAncestry(kind, context.ancestryCmdlines)) {
   425	    return {
   426	      allowed: false,
   427	      layer: 'ancestry',
   428	      reason: recursionReasonForLayer('ancestry'),
   429	      detail: `${kind} executor binary appears in process ancestry`,
   430	    };
   431	  }
   432	
   433	  if (detectFromRuntimeEnv(kind, env)) {
   434	    const envName = EXECUTOR_SESSION_ENV_BY_KIND[kind] ?? 'runtime session env';
   435	    return {
   436	      allowed: false,
   437	      layer: 'env',
   438	      reason: recursionReasonForLayer('env'),
   439	      detail: `${envName} is set for ${kind}`,
   440	    };
   441	  }
   442	
   443	  const statePaths = context.statePaths ?? getDefaultStatePaths(kind, env);
   444	  if (detectFromLockfile(kind, statePaths)) {
   445	    return {
   446	      allowed: false,
   447	      layer: 'lockfile',
   448	      reason: recursionReasonForLayer('lockfile'),
   449	      detail: `${kind} dispatch lockfile exists in runtime state path`,
   450	    };
   451	  }
   452	
   453	  return { allowed: true };
   454	}
   455	
   456	/**
   457	 * Build the environment for a dispatched executor.
   458	 *
   459	 * Filters the parent environment to only include allowed keys per executor kind
   460	 * and appends the current kind to the dispatch stack.
   461	 *
   462	 * @param config - Executor configuration.
   463	 * @param parentEnv - Parent environment (defaults to process.env).
   464	 * @returns Filtered environment with dispatch stack updated.
   465	 */
   466	export function buildExecutorDispatchEnv(
   467	  config: ExecutorConfig,
   468	  parentEnv: Record<string, string | undefined> = process.env,
   469	): Record<string, string | undefined> {
   470	  const kind = getExecutorKind(config);
   471	  if (kind === 'native') {
   472	    return { ...parentEnv };
   473	  }
   474	
   475	  const nextEnv: Record<string, string | undefined> = {};
   476	  for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(parentEnv)) {
   477	    if (value !== undefined && isAllowedExecutorEnvKey(key, kind)) {
   478	      nextEnv[key] = value;
   479	    }
   480	  }
   481	  nextEnv[CLI_DISPATCH_STACK_ENV] = [...splitDispatchStack(parentEnv[CLI_DISPATCH_STACK_ENV]), kind].join(':');
   482	  return nextEnv;
   483	}
   484	
   485	/**
   486	 * Build an executor audit record for provenance logging.
   487	 *
   488	 * @param executor - Executor configuration.
   489	 * @returns Audit record with kind, model, reasoning effort, and service tier.
   490	 */
   491	export function buildExecutorAuditRecord(executor: ExecutorConfig, lineageId?: string): Record<string, unknown> {
   492	  return {
   493	    kind: getExecutorKind(executor),
   494	    model: executor.model,
   495	    reasoningEffort: executor.reasoningEffort,
   496	    serviceTier: executor.serviceTier,
   497	    ...(lineageId !== undefined ? { lineageId } : {}),
   498	  };
   499	}
   500	
   501	/**
   502	 * Write the first executor provenance record for an iteration.
   503	 *
   504	 * Updates the iteration start record in the JSONL state log with executor
   505	 * audit data, or creates a new iteration_start record if none exists.
   506	 *
   507	 * @param stateLogPath - Path to the JSONL state log.
   508	 * @param executor - Executor configuration.
   509	 * @param iteration - Current iteration number.
   510	 */
   511	export function writeFirstRecordExecutor(stateLogPath: string, executor: ExecutorConfig, iteration: number): void {
   512	  if (getExecutorKind(executor) === 'native') {
   513	    return;
   514	  }
   515	
   516	  const { lines, hasTrailingNewline } = readJsonlFileAfterTailRepair(stateLogPath);
   517	  const recordIndex = findLatestIterationRecordIndex(lines, iteration);
   518	
   519	  if (recordIndex !== -1) {
   520	    const parsedRecord = JSON.parse(lines[recordIndex]);
   521	    if (!isObjectRecord(parsedRecord)) {
   522	      throw new Error(`Iteration ${iteration} JSONL record is not an object`);
   523	    }
   524	
   525	    if (isObjectRecord(parsedRecord.executor)) {
   526	      return;
   527	    }
   528	
   529	    lines[recordIndex] = JSON.stringify({
   530	      ...parsedRecord,
   531	      executor: buildExecutorAuditRecord(executor),
   532	    });
   533	    rewriteJsonlFile(stateLogPath, lines, hasTrailingNewline);
   534	    return;
   535	  }
   536	
   537	  appendJsonlRecordSafe(stateLogPath, {
   538	    type: 'iteration_start',
   539	    iteration,
   540	    executor: buildExecutorAuditRecord(executor),
   541	    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
   542	  });
   543	}
   544	
   545	/**
   546	 * Emit a dispatch failure event to the JSONL state log.
   547	 *
   548	 * @param stateLogPath - Path to the JSONL state log.
   549	 * @param executor - Executor configuration.
   550	 * @param reason - Dispatch failure reason.
   551	 * @param iteration - Current iteration number.
   552	 * @param detail - Optional failure detail string.
   553	 */
   554	export function emitDispatchFailure(
   555	  stateLogPath: string,
   556	  executor: ExecutorConfig,
   557	  reason: DispatchFailureReason,
   558	  iteration: number,
   559	  detail?: string,
   560	): void {
   561	  const lastIterationEvent = findLatestIterationEvent(readJsonlFileAfterTailRepair(stateLogPath).lines, iteration);
   562	  if (lastIterationEvent?.event === 'dispatch_failure') {
   563	    return;
   564	  }
   565	
   566	  appendJsonlRecordSafe(stateLogPath, {
   567	    type: 'event',
   568	    event: 'dispatch_failure',
   569	    ...(getExecutorKind(executor) === 'native' ? {} : { executor: buildExecutorAuditRecord(executor) }),
   570	    reason,
   571	    iteration,
   572	    ...(detail ? { detail } : {}),
   573	    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
   574	  });
   575	}
   576	
   577	/**
   578	 * Run a non-native executor command synchronously and log dispatch failures.
   579	 *
   580	 * Detects timeout, crash, and non-zero exit conditions and translates
   581	 * them into typed dispatch_failure JSONL events.
   582	 *
   583	 * @param input - Command input with executor config and state log path.
   584	 * @returns Always returns 0 (failures are logged, not thrown).
   585	 */
   586	export function runAuditedExecutorCommand(input: RunAuditedExecutorCommandInput): number {
   587	  const dispatchAllowed = validateExecutorDispatchAllowed(input.executor, input.guardContext);
   588	  if (!dispatchAllowed.allowed) {
   589	    emitDispatchFailure(input.stateLogPath, input.executor, dispatchAllowed.reason, input.iteration, dispatchAllowed.detail);
   590	    return 0;
   591	  }
   592	
   593	  const timeoutMs = Number.isFinite(input.timeoutSeconds)
   594	    ? Math.max(1000, Math.trunc(input.timeoutSeconds * 1000) - 1000)
   595	    : 1000;
   596	  const result = spawnSync(input.command, input.args, {
   597	    cwd: input.cwd,
   598	    encoding: 'utf8',
   599	    timeout: timeoutMs,
   600	    env: buildExecutorDispatchEnv(input.executor, input.guardContext?.env ?? process.env),
   601	    maxBuffer: 10 * 1024 * 1024,
   602	    ...(typeof input.input === 'string' ? { input: input.input } : {}),
   603	  });
   604	
   605	  if (typeof result.stdout === 'string' && result.stdout.length > 0) {
   606	    process.stdout.write(result.stdout);
   607	  }
   608	  if (typeof result.stderr === 'string' && result.stderr.length > 0) {
   609	    process.stderr.write(result.stderr);
   610	  }
   611	
   612	  if (result.error) {
   613	    const isTimeoutError =
   614	      result.error.name === 'TimeoutError' ||
   615	      (typeof result.error === 'object' &&
   616	        result.error !== null &&
   617	        'code' in result.error &&
   618	        result.error.code === 'ETIMEDOUT');
   619	    emitDispatchFailure(
   620	      input.stateLogPath,
   621	      input.executor,
   622	      isTimeoutError ? 'timeout' : 'crash',
   623	      input.iteration,
   624	      result.error.message,
   625	    );
   626	    return 0;
   627	  }
   628	
   629	  if (typeof result.status === 'number' && result.status !== 0) {
   630	    emitDispatchFailure(
   631	      input.stateLogPath,
   632	      input.executor,
   633	      'crash',
   634	      input.iteration,
   635	      `executor exited with status ${result.status}`,
   636	    );
   637	    return 0;
   638	  }
   639	
   640	  if (typeof result.signal === 'string' && result.signal.length > 0) {
   641	    emitDispatchFailure(
   642	      input.stateLogPath,
   643	      input.executor,
   644	      'crash',
   645	      input.iteration,
   646	      `executor terminated by signal ${result.signal}`,
   647	    );
   648	    return 0;
   649	  }
   650	
   651	  return 0;
   652	}
   653	
   654	/**
   655	 * Run a non-native executor command asynchronously with signal escalation.
   656	 *
   657	 * Uses spawn + detached process group for graceful timeout handling with
   658	 * SIGTERM escalation to SIGKILL after the grace period.
   659	 *
   660	 * @param input - Command input with executor config and state log path.
   661	 * @returns Promise resolving to 0 after process completes.
   662	 */
   663	export async function runAuditedExecutorCommandAsync(input: RunAuditedExecutorCommandInput): Promise<number> {
   664	  const dispatchAllowed = validateExecutorDispatchAllowed(input.executor, input.guardContext);
   665	  if (!dispatchAllowed.allowed) {
   666	    emitDispatchFailure(input.stateLogPath, input.executor, dispatchAllowed.reason, input.iteration, dispatchAllowed.detail);
   667	    return 0;
   668	  }
   669	
   670	  const timeoutMs = Number.isFinite(input.timeoutSeconds)
   671	    ? Math.max(1, Math.trunc(input.timeoutSeconds * 1000))
   672	    : 1000;
   673	  const graceMs = Number.isFinite(input.timeoutGraceMs)
   674	    ? Math.max(1, Math.trunc(input.timeoutGraceMs ?? 250))
   675	    : 250;
   676	
   677	  await new Promise<void>((resolve) => {
   678	    let timedOut = false;
   679	    let settled = false;
   680	    let graceTimer: NodeJS.Timeout | undefined;
   681	    const child = spawn(input.command, input.args, {
   682	      cwd: input.cwd,
   683	      detached: process.platform !== 'win32',
   684	      env: buildExecutorDispatchEnv(input.executor, input.guardContext?.env ?? process.env),
   685	      stdio: ['pipe', 'pipe', 'pipe'],
   686	    });
   687	
   688	    const timeoutTimer = setTimeout(() => {
   689	      timedOut = true;
   690	      killProcessGroup(child.pid, 'SIGTERM');
   691	      graceTimer = setTimeout(() => {
   692	        killProcessGroup(child.pid, 'SIGKILL');
   693	      }, graceMs);
   694	    }, timeoutMs);
   695	
   696	    if (typeof input.input === 'string') {
   697	      child.stdin.write(input.input);
   698	    }
   699	    child.stdin.end();
   700	
   701	    child.stdout.on('data', (chunk: Buffer) => {
   702	      process.stdout.write(chunk);
   703	    });
   704	    child.stderr.on('data', (chunk: Buffer) => {
   705	      process.stderr.write(chunk);
   706	    });
   707	
   708	    child.on('error', (error: Error) => {
   709	      if (settled) {
   710	        return;
   711	      }
   712	      settled = true;
   713	      clearTimeout(timeoutTimer);
   714	      if (graceTimer) clearTimeout(graceTimer);
   715	      emitDispatchFailure(input.stateLogPath, input.executor, 'crash', input.iteration, error.message);
   716	      resolve();
   717	    });
   718	
   719	    child.on('close', (status: number | null, signal: NodeJS.Signals | null) => {
   720	      if (settled) {
   721	        return;
   722	      }
   723	      settled = true;
   724	      clearTimeout(timeoutTimer);
   725	      if (graceTimer) clearTimeout(graceTimer);
   726	
   727	      if (timedOut) {
   728	        emitDispatchFailure(
   729	          input.stateLogPath,
   730	          input.executor,
   731	          'timeout',
   732	          input.iteration,
   733	          `executor timed out after ${timeoutMs}ms`,
   734	        );
   735	        resolve();
   736	        return;
   737	      }
   738	
   739	      if (typeof status === 'number' && status !== 0) {
   740	        emitDispatchFailure(
   741	          input.stateLogPath,
   742	          input.executor,
   743	          'crash',
   744	          input.iteration,
   745	          `executor exited with status ${status}`,
   746	        );
   747	        resolve();
   748	        return;
   749	      }
   750	
   751	      if (typeof signal === 'string' && signal.length > 0) {
   752	        emitDispatchFailure(
   753	          input.stateLogPath,
   754	          input.executor,
   755	          'crash',
   756	          input.iteration,
   757	          `executor terminated by signal ${signal}`,
   758	        );
   759	      }
   760	
   761	      resolve();
   762	    });
   763	  });
   764	
   765	  return 0;
   766	}
   767	
   768	/**
   769	 * Append executor audit information to the last JSONL record in the state log.
   770	 *
   771	 * @param stateLogPath - Path to the JSONL state log.
   772	 * @param executor - Executor configuration.
   773	 * @throws If the state log is empty or the last record is not an object.
   774	 */
   775	export function appendExecutorAuditToLastRecord(stateLogPath: string, executor: ExecutorConfig): void {
   776	  if (getExecutorKind(executor) === 'native') {
   777	    return;
   778	  }
   779	
   780	  const { lines, hasTrailingNewline } = readJsonlFile(stateLogPath);
   781	  const lastLineIndex = findLastNonEmptyLineIndex(lines);
   782	
   783	  if (lastLineIndex === -1) {
   784	    throw new Error('State log does not contain any JSONL records');
   785	  }
   786	
   787	  const parsedRecord = JSON.parse(lines[lastLineIndex]);
   788	  if (parsedRecord === null || Array.isArray(parsedRecord) || typeof parsedRecord !== 'object') {
   789	    throw new Error('Last JSONL record is not an object');
   790	  }
   791	
   792	  const mergedRecord: Record<string, unknown> = {
   793	    ...parsedRecord,
   794	    executor: buildExecutorAuditRecord(executor),
   795	  };
   796	
   797	  lines[lastLineIndex] = JSON.stringify(mergedRecord);
   798	  rewriteJsonlFile(stateLogPath, lines, hasTrailingNewline);
   799	}

 succeeded in 0ms:

    20	operating_principles:
    21	  core: "Fresh context per iteration + externalized state = no context degradation"
    22	  approach: "Dispatch focused review agent per dimension, evaluate convergence, repeat or stop"
    23	  mandate: "Never hold findings in memory; write everything to files. Review target is READ-ONLY."
    24	
    25	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    26	# USER INPUTS (from setup phase)
    27	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    28	user_inputs:
    29	  review_target: "[REVIEW_TARGET] - What to review. REQUIRED."
    30	  review_target_type: "[spec-folder|skill|agent|track|files] - Target type for scope derivation. REQUIRED."
    31	  review_dimensions: "[all|correctness,security,traceability,maintainability] - Dimensions to check. Default: all."
    32	  spec_folder: "[SPEC_FOLDER] - Spec folder path for state files. REQUIRED."
    33	  lineage_mode: "[auto|resume|restart] - Session lifecycle intent. Default: auto. The runtime only persists lineage events for `resume` (same sessionId, no archive) and `restart` (new sessionId, generation+1, prior review/ archived). `fork` and `completed-continue` are deferred and no longer accepted — see deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md §Lifecycle Branches."
    34	  max_iterations: 7
    35	  convergence_threshold: 0.10
    36	
    37	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    38	# CONTEXT LOADING
    39	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    40	context_loading:
    41	  trigger: "At workflow START, before phase_init"
    42	  purpose: "Load prior review context for the target and buffer it until strategy.md exists"
    43	  mcp_integration:
    44	    tool: memory_context
    45	    note: "Call MCP tools directly - NEVER through Code Mode"
    46	    parameters: { input: "{review_target}", mode: "focused", intent: "understand", includeContent: true }
    47	  when_to_skip: ["User says 'skip context' or 'fresh start'"]
    48	  behavior:
    49	    if_context_found: "Store full results as prior_context plus a concise prior_context_summary; inject after strategy.md is created."
    50	    if_no_context: "Set prior_context and prior_context_summary to 'None' and continue."
    51	
    52	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    53	# SKILL REFERENCE
    54	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    55	skill_reference:
    56	  skill: deep-review
    57	  skill_md: .opencode/skills/deep-review/SKILL.md
    58	  references:
    59	    loop_protocol: .opencode/skills/deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md
    60	    state_format: .opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_format.md
    61	    convergence: .opencode/skills/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md
    62	  templates:
    63	    config: .opencode/skills/deep-review/assets/deep_review_config.json
    64	    strategy: .opencode/skills/deep-review/assets/deep_review_strategy.md
    65	
    66	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    67	# AGENT CONFIGURATION
    68	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    69	agent_config:
    70	  agent: deep-review
    71	  agent_file: ".opencode/agents/deep-review.md"
    72	  # Canonical path. Runtime-specific directories (e.g. .claude/agents/) reference this source.
    73	  mode: subagent
    74	  model: opus
    75	  leaf_only: true
    76	  tools: [Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob]
    77	  mcp_servers: [mk-spec-memory]
    78	  tool_call_budget: { target: 9, soft_max: 12, hard_max: 13 }
    79	
    80	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    81	# STATE FILE PATHS
    82	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    83	state_paths:
    84	  # Template paths document the canonical packet layout. Runtime resolution happens via
    85	  # step_resolve_artifact_root plus the shared resolver, so root specs keep
    86	  # root-local review/ and child phases keep local review/ packet dirs.
    87	  # Canonical root-spec write targets remain review/deep-review-config.json,
    88	  # review/deep-review-state.jsonl, and review/deep-review-findings-registry.json.
    89	  packet_dir: "{artifact_dir}"
    90	  local_packet_dir: "{spec_folder}/review"
    91	  legacy_root_report: "{spec_folder}/review-report.md"
    92	  config: "{artifact_dir}/deep-review-config.json"
    93	  state_log: "{artifact_dir}/deep-review-state.jsonl"
    94	  findings_registry: "{artifact_dir}/deep-review-findings-registry.json"
    95	  strategy: "{artifact_dir}/deep-review-strategy.md"
    96	  dashboard: "{artifact_dir}/deep-review-dashboard.md"
    97	  pause_sentinel: "{artifact_dir}/.deep-review-pause"
    98	  prompt_dir: "{artifact_dir}/prompts"
    99	  iteration_dir: "{artifact_dir}/iterations"
   100	  iteration_pattern: "{artifact_dir}/iterations/iteration-{NNN}.md"
   101	  # Per-iteration structured delta stream — complement to the canonical state_log
   102	  # append. MUST contain at least one record with `type === "iteration"`; variants
   103	  # like `"iteration_delta"` are silently dropped by the reducer.
   104	  delta_dir: "{artifact_dir}/deltas"
   105	  delta_pattern: "{artifact_dir}/deltas/iter-{NNN}.jsonl"
   106	  review_output: "{artifact_dir}/review-report.md"
   107	  resource_map_output: "{artifact_dir}/resource-map.md"
   108	  archive_root: "{artifact_archive_root}"
   109	
   110	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   111	# WORKFLOW
   112	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   113	workflow:
   114	
   115	  step_resolve_artifact_root:
   116	    description: "Resolve artifact directory — use fan-out lineage override when provided, else resolve from spec_folder"
   117	    note: "Root specs keep review artifacts directly in {spec_folder}/review/. Child phases and sub-phases keep local packet directories in {spec_folder}/review/{packet}/, and archives stay local at {spec_folder}/review_archive/{packet}/."
   118	    branch_on: "config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir"
   119	    if_present:
   120	      bind:
   121	        artifact_dir: "{config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir}"
   122	        artifact_root_dir: "{config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir}"
   123	        artifact_subfolder: null
   124	        artifact_archive_root: "{config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir}/_archive"
   125	    if_absent:
   126	      command: "node -e \"const { resolveArtifactRoot } = require('.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/shared/review-research-paths.cjs'); console.log(JSON.stringify(resolveArtifactRoot('{spec_folder}', 'review')));\""
   127	      outputs:
   128	        - artifact_root_dir: "Resolved local review owner directory"
   129	        - artifact_subfolder: "Local packet subfolder ({ownerSlug}-pt-{NN}), else null"
   130	        - artifact_dir: "Resolved review packet directory for this target"
   131	        - artifact_archive_root: "Resolved local review archive directory for this target"
   132	
   133	  step_fanout_spawn:
   134	    description: "Spawn fan-out lineages; skip and proceed to phase_init normally when single-executor"
   135	    skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"
   136	    instructions: |
   137	      Fan-out mode is active. Spawn all lineages. Each lineage runs the full loop in its own
   138	      isolated sub-packet under {artifact_dir}/lineages/{label}/.
   139	      After all lineages complete, jump directly to phase_synthesis — skip phase_init and
   140	      phase_main_loop entirely (those are the single-executor path). The merge step in
   141	      phase_synthesis will consolidate results across all lineages.
   142	    sub_steps:
   143	      step_fanout_spawn_cli:
   144	        description: "Spawn all CLI lineages in capped worker pool"
   145	        skip_when: "no entries in config.fanout.executors with kind != 'native'"
   146	        command: |
   147	          node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs \
   148	            --spec-folder {spec_folder} \
   149	            --loop-type review \
   150	            --fanout-config-json '{config.fanout_json}' \
   151	            --base-artifact-dir {artifact_dir}
   152	        working_directory: "{repo_root}"
   153	        wait_for_completion: true
   154	      step_fanout_spawn_native:
   155	        description: "Dispatch each native lineage as a sequential deep-review sub-agent"
   156	        skip_when: "no entries in config.fanout.executors with kind == 'native'"
   157	        for_each: "lineage in expanded native entries of config.fanout"
   158	        dispatch:
   159	          agent: deep-review
   160	          model: opus
   161	          context: |
   162	            Execute the deep-review loop with these parameters:
   163	              spec_folder: {spec_folder}
   164	              config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir: {artifact_dir}/lineages/{lineage.label}
   165	              session_id: fanout-{lineage.label}-{fanout_run_id}
   166	              executor: native
   167	            Run to convergence. Write all outputs to the override directory.
   168	        wait_for_completion: true
   169	
   170	  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   171	  # PHASE 1: INITIALIZATION
   172	  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   173	  phase_init:
   174	    purpose: Create all state files for the review session and resolve review scope
   175	    steps:
   176	
   177	      step_preflight_contract:
   178	        action: "Validate required setup bindings and executable workflow contract before any file writes"
   179	        verify:
   180	          - required_values_present: [review_target, review_target_type, review_dimensions, spec_folder, max_iterations, convergence_threshold]
   181	          - spec_folder_is_within: "specs/ or .opencode/specs/ (canonicalize alias roots before validation)"
   182	          - unresolved_placeholders: "FAIL if any user-facing template references placeholders not produced by earlier steps"
   183	          - tool_contract: "FAIL if executable steps reference tools outside the command allowlist"
   184	        on_fail: "Halt with STATUS=FAIL and report the missing binding or unsupported contract reference"
   185	
   186	      step_create_directories:
   187	        action: "Create spec folder and canonical review packet directories if they don't exist"
   188	        command: "mkdir -p {state_paths.prompt_dir} {state_paths.iteration_dir} {state_paths.delta_dir}"
   189	
   190	      step_detect_resource_map:
   191	        action: "Detect resource-map.md before config creation so init can persist coverage-gate state"
   192	        inspect:
   193	          - "{spec_folder}/resource-map.md"
   194	        classify:
   195	          present: "resource-map.md exists at init"
   196	          absent: "resource-map.md does not exist at init"
   197	        outputs:
   198	          - resource_map_present: "Boolean true when {spec_folder}/resource-map.md exists, else false"
   199	          - resource_map_known_context: "When present: concise resource-map snapshot for Known Context and charter framing. When absent: 'resource-map.md not present; skipping coverage gate'."
   200	
   201	      step_migrate_legacy_review_state:
   202	        action: "Rehome legacy review artifacts into review/iterations before session classification"
   203	        detect:
   204	          canonical_review_state:
   205	            - "{state_paths.config}"
   206	            - "{state_paths.state_log}"
   207	            - "{state_paths.findings_registry}"
   208	            - "{state_paths.strategy}"
   209	          legacy_review_state:
   210	            - "{spec_folder}/scratch/deep-research-config.json"
   211	            - "{spec_folder}/scratch/deep-research-state.jsonl"
   212	            - "{spec_folder}/scratch/deep-review-strategy.md"
   213	            - "{spec_folder}/scratch/deep-review-dashboard.md"
   214	            - "{spec_folder}/scratch/iteration-*.md"
   215	            - "{spec_folder}/scratch/.deep-research-pause"
   216	            - "{state_paths.local_packet_dir}/iteration-*.md"
   217	            - "{state_paths.legacy_root_report}"
   218	          legacy_review_markers:
   219	            - "scratch/deep-review-strategy.md exists"
   220	            - "scratch/deep-research-config.json declares mode == 'review' OR scratch/deep-research-state.jsonl contains mode == 'review'"
   221	        migrate_only:
   222	          - "{spec_folder}/scratch/deep-research-config.json -> {state_paths.config}"
   223	          - "{spec_folder}/scratch/deep-research-state.jsonl -> {state_paths.state_log}"
   224	          - "{spec_folder}/scratch/deep-review-strategy.md -> {state_paths.strategy}"
   225	          - "{spec_folder}/scratch/deep-review-dashboard.md -> {state_paths.dashboard}"
   226	          - "{spec_folder}/scratch/iteration-*.md -> {state_paths.iteration_dir}/"
   227	          - "{spec_folder}/scratch/.deep-research-pause -> {state_paths.pause_sentinel}"
   228	          - "{state_paths.local_packet_dir}/iteration-*.md -> {state_paths.iteration_dir}/"
   229	          - "{state_paths.legacy_root_report} -> {state_paths.review_output}"
   230	        on_canonical_present: "Leave canonical review/ state untouched and do not overwrite it with legacy scratch artifacts."
   231	        on_legacy_detected: "Move only the review-specific whitelist, append a migration event to the canonical JSONL, and leave unrelated scratch files untouched."
   232	        on_conflict: "If canonical review/ state and legacy scratch review state both exist but disagree, halt for operator repair instead of merging contradictory state."
   233	        command: "mkdir -p {state_paths.iteration_dir} && if [ ! -e {state_paths.config} ] && [ ! -e {state_paths.state_log} ] && [ ! -e {state_paths.strategy} ] && [ -e {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-review-strategy.md ] && ( [ -e {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-research-config.json ] || [ -e {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-research-state.jsonl ] ); then [ -e {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-research-config.json ] && mv {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-research-config.json {state_paths.config}; [ -e {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-research-state.jsonl ] && mv {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-research-state.jsonl {state_paths.state_log}; [ -e {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-review-strategy.md ] && mv {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-review-strategy.md {state_paths.strategy}; [ -e {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-review-dashboard.md ] && mv {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-review-dashboard.md {state_paths.dashboard}; find {spec_folder}/scratch -maxdepth 1 -name 'iteration-*.md' -exec mv {} {state_paths.iteration_dir}/ \\; ; [ -e {spec_folder}/scratch/.deep-research-pause ] && mv {spec_folder}/scratch/.deep-research-pause {state_paths.pause_sentinel}; [ -e {state_paths.legacy_root_report} ] && mv {state_paths.legacy_root_report} {state_paths.review_output}; if [ -e {state_paths.state_log} ]; then printf '%s\n' '{\"type\":\"event\",\"event\":\"migration\",\"mode\":\"review\",\"legacyArtifacts\":[\"deep-research-config.json\",\"deep-research-state.jsonl\",\".deep-research-pause\"],\"canonicalArtifacts\":[\"deep-review-config.json\",\"deep-review-state.jsonl\",\".deep-review-pause\"],\"timestamp\":\"{ISO_8601_NOW}\"}' >> {state_paths.state_log}; fi; fi; find {state_paths.local_packet_dir} -maxdepth 1 -name 'iteration-*.md' -exec mv {} {state_paths.iteration_dir}/ \\; 2>/dev/null || true"
   234	
   235	      step_classify_session:
   236	        action: "Classify the session before writing files"
   237	        inspect:
   238	          - "{state_paths.config}"
   239	          - "{state_paths.state_log}"
   240	          - "{state_paths.findings_registry}"
   241	          - "{state_paths.strategy}"
   242	        classify:
   243	          fresh: "None of the three state artifacts exist"
   244	          resume: "Config, JSONL, and strategy all exist and config.mode == 'review' and agree on target/spec folder"
   245	          completed-session: "Resume artifacts exist and config.status == 'complete'"
   246	          invalid-state: "Any partial, missing, or contradictory combination"
   247	        outputs:
   248	          - session_state: "fresh | resume | completed-session | invalid-state"
   249	        on_resume:
   250	          log: "Auto-resume: state artifacts are complete and consistent. Continue the same lineage (sessionId unchanged, generation unchanged, no archive)."
   251	          append_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"resumed","mode":"review","sessionId":"{session_id}","parentSessionId":"{session_id}","lineageMode":"resume","continuedFromRun":{iteration_count},"generation":{generation},"archivedPath":null,"timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}"}'
   252	          skip_to: phase_loop
   253	        on_restart:
   254	          action: "Archive the current review packet under the root-level review_archive/ tree and start a new lineage segment with a fresh sessionId and generation+1."
   255	          command: "if [ -d {state_paths.packet_dir} ]; then mkdir -p {state_paths.archive_root} && mv {state_paths.packet_dir} {state_paths.archive_root}/{timestamp_slug}; fi"
   256	          set:
   257	            lineage_mode: "restart"
   258	            parent_session_id: "{prior_session_id}"
   259	            generation: "{prior_generation + 1}"
   260	          append_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"restarted","mode":"review","sessionId":"{session_id}","parentSessionId":"{prior_session_id}","lineageMode":"restart","generation":{generation},"archivedPath":"{state_paths.archive_root}/{timestamp_slug}","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}"}'
   261	          skip_to: step_create_config
   262	        note_deferred_branches: "fork and completed-continue are deferred and no longer runtime-supported in this release. The reducer still reads `lineageMode` from config for dashboards and analysis, but only `new`, `resume`, and `restart` are live. See deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md §Lifecycle Branches for the one-session contract."
   263	        on_completed_session:
   264	          log: "Prior session already completed. Either archive the completed review/ tree and start a fresh session, or jump straight to synthesis with the existing artifacts."
   265	          skip_to: phase_synthesis
   266	        on_invalid_state:
   267	          halt: true
   268	          message: "Existing deep-review state in review/ is incomplete or contradictory. Repair or archive the invalid review packet before continuing."
   269	        on_fresh:
   270	          action: "Proceed with a new session"
   271	
   272	      step_scope_discovery:
   273	        action: "Resolve review target to a concrete file list based on target type"
   274	        resolve:
   275	          spec-folder: |
   276	            1. Read spec.md, plan.md, tasks.md, checklist.md, implementation-summary.md
   277	            2. Discover implementation files referenced in spec artifacts
   278	            3. Discover test files associated with implementation
   279	            4. Set scope = all discovered files
   280	          skill: |

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "rg -n \"fanout|fan-out|artifact_dir_override|artifact-dir-override|executors|concurrency|skip_when|if_absent|config\\.executor|config\\.fanout\" .opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml .opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_confirm.yaml .opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml .opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
